rows.notes: Clear centered year at top of page; no amounts present and perfectly legible.
Clear script heading 'Soluta.' directly under the year; no monetary values.
Top section label. Handwriting for 'Officiaruis' shows an older-style long 's' but text is legible; no amounts present.

rows.description: Stipend: ad Fest. Natv: 1824. et Off - - - -
rows.notes: Monetary columns well aligned against ruling lines; pounds/shillings/pence read clearly as 10/9/10. No fraction symbol present.
Description legible. Amount columns clearly show 22 pounds, 17 shillings, 4 pence; digits aligned and within valid ranges.
Name 'Dna Thiers' legible. Amount 31/2/3 clearly in columns; no fraction mark.
Description contains abbreviations. Amount is a round 56 pounds 0s 0d; alignment with pound column is clear.
Short description 'Dno Rectori' then dashes. Amount 30/9/2 is clearly written and aligned.
'Dit. Merton' heading for the next line; amount 83/4/9 is neatly aligned and legible.

rows.description: d. d. ad Fest. S. Mich.
rows.notes: Abbreviated description 'd. d. ad Fest. S. Mich.' Amount columns read 83/4/6 clearly; alignment consistent with above lines.
New-year (1825) stipend line; amount 30/3/6 is well-placed in the three monetary columns.

Amount reads 40 pounds, 10 shillings, 9 pence with a clear halfpenny sign (looped 'ob') after the pence; columns alignment is consistent. Slight uncertainty only on interpreting the halfpenny symbol (read as 0.5).
Repeated 'Dno Rectori. ut supra.' with clear amount 30/9/2; digits well-formed and aligned.

rows.description: Stipend: ad Fest. D. Joan: Bapt: et off.
rows.notes: Amount appears as 17 pounds, 14 shillings, 0 pence with a small looped mark after the pence column. That mark likely indicates a halfpenny (0.5) but is faint; chosen as 0.5 based on similarity to other clearly marked halfpennies on the page. Description is legible.

Amount reads 31/2/2 with a small additional mark after pence resembling '3q' (three farthings). Interpreted as three farthings = 0.75; the farthing-style mark is reasonably clear but slightly cramped, hence slightly reduced confidence.

Description contains several abbreviations and an unclear surname/word ('Huld' is a best reading of the script). Amount columns show 52/5/4 but the handwriting around the description is less clear; monetary digits themselves are readable. Moderate uncertainty due to unclear words in description.

Amount reads 13/12/11 with a small quarter-farthing style mark after the pence interpreted as a farthing (0.25). Column positions are consistent; description legible though abbreviated ('4ta' = quarta).
Repeat line 'Dno Rectori. ut supra.' with amount 30/9/2 clearly written and aligned.

rows.description: Stipend: Guimar: ad Fest: S. Mich.
rows.notes: 'Guimar' reading is clear in context. Amount 23/16/0 is plainly written in the monetary columns.
Amount appears as 30/1/3 with a clear halfpenny mark (loop) after the pence. The place/name 'Whittinham' is legible though written in a flowing hand; alignment of numbers is consistent.
Another repeat of 'Dno Rectori. ut supra.' with cleanly written 30/9/2; no fraction.
Centered subheading. The final word 'pmi' (or 'pmi' abbreviation) is somewhat abbreviated/unclear; no amounts on this line.
Amount reads 0 pounds, 17 shillings, 9 pence with a clear halfpenny mark after the pence. 'Pro Fine Meland' reading is legible; columns aligned.

Final underlined total at bottom right reads 673 pounds, 7 shillings, 10 pence with a small quarter-farthing mark (interpreted as 0.25). Number alignment is clear; the small fraction mark is slightly faint but consistent with quarter notation elsewhere.
